title: Burden of proof; civil proceedings
doc_html:
Art. 1105. Burden of proof; civil proceedings

            A. In any civil proceeding in which the defense of self-defense is raised, the defendant shall have the burden to prove by a preponderance of the evidence that he acted in self-defense.

            B. For the purposes of this Article, if the defense of self-defense is raised in a civil proceeding by an authorized person as defined in R.S. 9:2793.12, the burden of proof shall be on the party asserting the action to prove by a preponderance of the evidence that the injury, death, or loss complained of was not caused by a justified use of force or self-defense by the authorized person.

            Acts 2024, No. 729, §1.
doc_text:
Art. 1105.  Burden of proof; civil proceedings
A.  In any civil proceeding in which the defense of self-defense is raised, the
defendant shall have the burden to prove by a preponderance of the evidence that he acted
in self-defense.
B.  For the purposes of this Article, if the defense of self-defense is raised in a civil
proceeding by an authorized person as defined in R.S. 9:2793.12, the burden of proof shall
be on the party asserting the action to prove by a preponderance of the evidence that the
injury, death, or loss complained of was not caused by a justified use of force or self-defense
by the authorized person.
Acts 2024, No. 729, §1.
